summary: The main claim is: OpenAI names building superintelligence as one of its primary goals.

The cynical reading of Altman’s policy tour, therefore, is as a Machiavellian advertisement for the usage of AI, one that benefits not just OpenAI but also other companies peddling “superintelligence,” like Anthropic.
The main claim is: + Develop a risk threshold through more research and funding into safety evaluations.
The main claim is: An element of a mind is explicit when it is available to relate to other elements when suitable.
The main claim is: That's just a standard two-player zero-sum game between the agent and the adversary, so we can import all the nice intuitive stuff from game theory.
In the existing presentations of infra-bayes, the two-player game is only given implicitly.

top_comment.author: leogao
top_comment.text: Adding $200 to the pool. Also, I endorse the existence of more bounties/contests like this.

summary: The main claim is: Just like every component of the world-model would need to interact with each other, so would they need to be able to interact with the planner.
That is, should we actually expect AIs to learn a module we can reasonably describe as a "world-model"?
The main claim is: I suspect that the way burnout manifests in the rationalist community is uniquely screwed up.
I in no way mean to imply that this is the only mechanism by which burnout happens in our community, but I think it's probably a pretty big one.
The main claim is: evolution is also a program search; the programs are genes/beings.
(in the same way, markets is a human-level intelligent program search; the programs are companies trying to do new things.
The main claim is: Let's imagine a toy environment where reward is a function of the value of just one node, xi.
Given full knowledge of the environment structure, its current state, and the reward nodes, what can we say about the optimal policy?
The main claim is: The ultimate goal is to get a reward model that represents human preferences for how a task should be done: this is also known as Inverse Reinforcement Learning.
A key advantage of RLHF is the ease of gathering feedback and the sample efficiency required to train the reward model.

The main claim is: Different capabilities groups are propelled at least partly by "if we don't do this, another group will anyway", or the subtly different "we must do this, or another group will first".
